subject: English / First Flight
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the main events and imagery of the poem
Recognize the movement and vitality of trees as described by the poet
Develop basic comprehension and summarization skills
Appreciate the poet’s depiction of nature and human observation
Encourage creative expression through writing and drawing

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Poem and Poet
topics_covered: Basic understanding of the poem’s imagery
teaching_strategy: Introduce Adrienne Rich and her style in simple terms
Ask students to observe trees around them and describe their movement
student_activities: Discuss the title and predict what the poem might describe
Share experiences of watching nature or trees
assessment: Oral Q&A on poet and poem title

lesson_plan.title: Reading and Comprehension – Observing the Trees
topics_covered: Explain difficult words: veranda, lichen, disengage
Understand the trees’ movement and personification
teaching_strategy: Read line by line and explain simply
Encourage students to visualize the trees moving
student_activities: Underline phrases showing movement of trees
Summarize what the trees do in 2–3 sentences
assessment: Ask students to describe the trees’ actions

lesson_plan.title: Imagery and Human Observation
topics_covered: Contrast between human observation and natural action
Use of night, moon, and sensory details
teaching_strategy: Explain how Rich describes the trees’ autonomy
Highlight the imagery of moonlight and movement
student_activities: Identify words and phrases showing the trees’ activity
Discuss how the observer reacts in the poem
assessment: Oral Q&A on imagery and observer’s role
Check students’ explanations in simple words

lesson_plan.title: Creative Reflection and Expression
topics_covered: Relating the poem to personal experiences of nature
Expressing thoughts creatively through writing and drawing
Reflecting on the vitality and autonomy of trees
teaching_strategy: Encourage students to connect the poem to what they see outdoors
Summarize key points of movement, imagery, and observation on the board
student_activities: Write a short paragraph imagining trees moving at night
Draw a simple illustration of trees moving out into the forest
assessment: Class discussion on lessons learned about nature and observation

teaching_aids: Printed or projected text of 'The Trees'
Images of trees, forests, moonlit night
Charts highlighting imagery and human observation
Flashcards with keywords and difficult words
evaluation_methods: Group discussion on imagery and themes
teacher_tip: Keep explanations simple and interactive. Emphasize the vivid imagery of trees moving independently. Encourage students to observe nature and relate it to the poem for better understanding.
